brief_justification: Paper A's machinery is a multi-scale stochastic/diffusion PDE (Persson's theory) that propagates the probability density of contact pressure and interfacial gap across magnification scales, whereas Paper B's machinery is deterministic queueing/capacity analysis of bidirectional traffic at one-lane bottlenecks (FIFO vs directional-priority policies, delay and capacity-boundary formulas). There is no role-for-role correspondence between a scale-evolving Fokker-Planck-type density and a traffic-flow queueing capacity model, so they do not share an underlying method.
